title: lonchocarpus eriocarinalis
intro: Lonchocarpus eriocarinalis, also known as the Mexican hairless heartwood tree, is a plant species native to Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus eriocarinalis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Lonchocarpus eriocarinalis can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and are often used in traditional folk medicine in regions where the plant is native.
